revision.text: '''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。

'''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。
[[Category:福岡県の神社|とあけしんしや]]

'''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。 ==歴史==
[[Category:福岡県の神社|とあけしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=戸明神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地| 位置=| 祭神=天手力雄大神
天児屋根大神| 社格=村社| 例祭=[[10月8日]] - [[10月9日]]| 神事=節分厄除祭　豆まき神事}} '''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== ==歴史== ==年中行事== [[Category:福岡県の神社|とあけしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=戸明神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地| 位置=| 祭神=天手力雄大神
天児屋根大神| 社格=村社| 例祭=[[10月8日]] - [[10月9日]]| 神事=節分厄除祭　豆まき神事}} '''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== 天の岩戸神話に登場する天手力雄大神・天児屋根大神を祀る。 ==歴史== ==年中行事== [[Category:福岡県の神社|とあけしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=戸明神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地| 位置=| 祭神=天手力雄大神
天児屋根大神| 社格=村社| 例祭=[[10月8日]] - [[10月9日]]| 神事=節分厄除祭　豆まき神事}} '''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== 天の岩戸神話に登場する天手力雄大神・天児屋根大神を祀る。 ==歴史== 　戸明神社は、安屋・有毛・大鳥居・高須に鎮座される「戸明神社」の本宮で、稲国・岩屋・脇田・竹並・払川・塩屋・小敷に鎮座の「産土氏神社」18社の総社として古来崇敬されてきた。
　その創建の時期については不明であるが、当社第6代宮司幡掛元成の誌す『戸明神社縁起書序』（天文5年）に「昔、当社は岩屋・柏原との間、戸明浜に御社有りて甚だ厳しき宮造りなりしを、享禄の年頃此の地に移し奉るなり」とあり、これによれば当社が戸明浜から現鎮座地の蜑住に遷座されたのはほぼ享禄（1528年）の頃であると共に、これ以前に創建の時期をうかがう事ができる。
　また、『福岡県神社誌』（昭和19年）によれば、「往昔は大社にて郷内の三大社と称し、島郷30余村より春秋両季五穀成就の祈祷は勿論、毎年臨時祭絶えず執行せり」とあり、宮司家が郡内神職の頭取に任じてきた事と併せ考え、昔時の繁栄が偲ばれる。 ==年中行事== [[Category:福岡県の神社|とあけしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=戸明神社| 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地| 位置=| 祭神=天手力雄大神
天児屋根大神| 社格=村社| 例祭=[[10月8日]] - [[10月9日]]| 神事=節分厄除祭　豆まき神事}} '''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、[[天手力雄大神]]・[[天児屋根大神]]を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== 天の岩戸神話に登場する天手力雄大神・天児屋根大神を祀る。 ==歴史== 　戸明神社は、安屋・有毛・大鳥居・高須に鎮座される「戸明神社」の本宮で、稲国・岩屋・脇田・竹並・払川・塩屋・小敷に鎮座の「産土氏神社」18社の総社として古来崇敬されてきた。
　その創建の時期については不明であるが、戸明神社第6代宮司幡掛元成の誌す『戸明神社縁起書序』（天文5年）に「昔、当社は岩屋・柏原との間、戸明浜に御社有りて甚だ厳しき宮造りなりしを、享禄の年頃此の地に移し奉るなり」とあり、これによれば当社が戸明浜から現鎮座地の蜑住に遷座されたのはほぼ享禄（1528年）の頃であると共に、享禄以前には既に創建していたと考える事ができる。
　また、『福岡県神社誌』（昭和19年）によれば、「往昔は大社にて郷内の三大社と称し、島郷30余村より春秋両季五穀成就の祈祷は勿論、毎年臨時祭絶えず執行せり」とあり、宮司家が郡内神職の頭取に任じてきた事と併せ考え、昔時の繁栄が偲ばれる。 ==年中行事== [[Category:福岡県の神社|とあけしんしや]]

'''Togami Shrine'''
[[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{Image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:{{神社|名称=高向神社 |画像=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Togami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:{{神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:{{神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:{{神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

[[蛭子神]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[保食神]]
[[菊理媛神|白山姫命]]
[[菅原道真]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }}]]

The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:{{神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:{{神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:11]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}}

{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|12=draft}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|12=draft}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|12=draft}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|12=draft}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|12=draft}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)|12=draft}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社|12=draft}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}}

[[Sugawara no Michizane]] |社格= |創建=不詳 |本殿=三間社[[切妻造]] |別名= 五社明神（旧称） |札所等= |例祭=10月10日に獅子舞、地車 |神事= }}]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:神社|名称=高向神社 |image=[[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Togami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}}

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:戸明神社 up to rev 79507045
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} {{神社 | 名称=戸明神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=北九州市}} | 所在地=福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | 祭神=天手力雄大神（[[アメノタヂカラオ]]）
天児屋根大神（[[アメノコヤネ]]） | 社格=村社 | 例祭=[[10月8日]] - [[10月9日]] | 神事=節分厄除祭　豆まき神事 }} '''戸明神社'''（とあけじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[若松区]]にある[[神社]]。日本神話に登場する神、天手力雄大神（[[アメノタヂカラオ]]）・天児屋根大神（[[アメノコヤネ]]）を主祭神として祀る、福岡県下では珍しい[[神社]]である。 ==祭神== 天の岩戸神話に登場する天手力雄大神（[[アメノタヂカラオ]]）・天児屋根大神（[[アメノコヤネ]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== 戸明神社は、安屋・有毛・大鳥居・[[高須 (北九州市)|高須]]に鎮座される「戸明神社」の本宮で、稲国・岩屋・脇田・竹並・払川・塩屋・小敷に鎮座の「産土氏神社」18社の総社として古来崇敬されてきた。 その創建の時期については不明であるが、戸明神社第6代宮司幡掛元成の誌す『戸明神社縁起書序』（天文5年）に「昔、当社は岩屋・柏原との間、戸明浜に御社有りて甚だ厳しき宮造りなりしを、享禄の年頃此の地に移し奉るなり」とあり、これによれば当社が戸明浜から現鎮座地の蜑住に遷座されたのはほぼ享禄（1528年）の頃であると共に、享禄以前には既に創建されていたと考える事ができる。 また、『福岡県神社誌』（昭和19年）によれば、「往昔は大社にて郷内の三大社と称し、島郷30余村より春秋両季五穀成就の祈祷は勿論、毎年臨時祭絶えず執行せり」とあり、宮司家が郡内神職の頭取に任じてきた事と併せ考え、昔時の繁栄が偲ばれる。 ==外部リンク== *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=戸明神社公式サイト |date=20071230053813}}{{リンク切れ|date=2018年5月14日 (月) 08:41 (UTC)|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/というサイトが存在するが、住所が微妙に違う。}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とあけしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:北九州市の神社]] [[Category:若松区の歴史]] [[Category:若松区の建築物]]

{{Cite sources|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Former village shrines of Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 8|qq=}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 9|qq=}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|qq=}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu, Kitakyushu|qq=}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami Shrines|qq=}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 9月}} {{Shrine | name = Toake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=北九州市}} | location = 福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Okami]]
[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Okami]] | type = Village Shrine (旧村社) | festival = [[October 8]]–[[October 9]] | Rituals = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival (Bean-throwing Ritual) }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], specifically in the [[Wakamatsu-ku|Wakamatsu Ward]]. The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] and [[Ame-no-Koyane]], two deities featured prominently in Japanese mythology. It holds significance as a rare shrine dedicated to these gods in the Fukuoka region. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine is dedicated to: * '''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao]]''': A god of strength, known for his role in the ''[[Ama-no-Iwato]]'' (Heavenly Rock Cave) myth, where he pulled open the rock door to coax out [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''': A deity associated with rituals and ceremonies, who played a key role in the same myth by reciting prayers to invite Amaterasu's return. == History == The shrine serves as the principal shrine (本宮) for a network of shrines in the surrounding areas, including those in Anya, Aruge, Ōtorii, and Takasu. It is also recognized as the chief shrine (総社) for 18 "Ubusuna-sha" (産土氏神社) in neighboring regions such as Inakuni, Iwaya, and Wakata. == Foundation == The exact date of the shrine's foundation is unknown. However, the ''Enkisho'' (縁起書) written by the sixth-generation head priest, '''Hatakae Motonari''', in 1536 (天文5th year ), provides some clues. It states that the shrine was originally located at Toakehama and later moved to its current location in [[Kyōroku]] (享禄, 1528–1532). == Historical Significance == According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Record'' (昭和19th year ), Toake Shrine was once a significant shrine, referred to as one of the "Three Great Shrines" of the region. It hosted seasonal festivals for the prosperity of agriculture and other rites for the local villages, demonstrating its historical importance as a central religious site. == Annual Events == The shrine is known for its annual festivals and rituals, including: * '''October 8–9:''' Autumn Festival (festival) * '''Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival:''' A bean-throwing ritual conducted for warding off evil spirits and bringing good fortune. == External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071230053813/http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ Archived Toake Shrine Website] (Japanese, archived) * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tomei Shrine]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tomei Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Tomei Shrine]] [[sn:omei Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 9月}} {{Shrine | name = Toake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=北九州市}} | location = 福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Okami]]
[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Okami]] | type = Village Shrine (旧村社) | festival = [[October 8]]–[[October 9]] | Rituals = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival (Bean-throwing Ritual) }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Kitakyushu]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], specifically in the [[Wakamatsu-ku|Wakamatsu Ward]]. The shrine enshrines [[Ame-no-Tajikarao]] and [[Ame-no-Koyane]], two deities featured prominently in Japanese mythology. It holds significance as a rare shrine dedicated to these gods in the Fukuoka region. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine is dedicated to: * '''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao]]''': A god of strength, known for his role in the ''[[Ama-no-Iwato]]'' (Heavenly Rock Cave) myth, where he pulled open the rock door to coax out [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''': A deity associated with rituals and ceremonies, who played a key role in the same myth by reciting prayers to invite Amaterasu's return. == History == The shrine serves as the principal shrine (本宮) for a network of shrines in the surrounding areas, including those in Anya, Aruge, Ōtorii, and Takasu. It is also recognized as the chief shrine (総社) for 18 "Ubusuna-sha" (産土氏神社) in neighboring regions such as Inakuni, Iwaya, and Wakata. == Foundation == The exact date of the shrine's foundation is unknown. However, the ''Enkisho'' (縁起書) written by the sixth-generation head priest, '''Hatakae Motonari''', in 1536 (天文5th year ), provides some clues. It states that the shrine was originally located at Toakehama and later moved to its current location in [[Kyōroku]] (享禄, 1528–1532). == Historical Significance == According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Record'' (昭和19th year ), Toake Shrine was once a significant shrine, referred to as one of the "Three Great Shrines" of the region. It hosted seasonal festivals for the prosperity of agriculture and other rites for the local villages, demonstrating its historical importance as a central religious site. == Annual Events == The shrine is known for its annual festivals and rituals, including: * '''October 8–9:''' Autumn Festival (festival) * '''Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival:''' A bean-throwing ritual conducted for warding off evil spirits and bringing good fortune. == External Links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20071230053813/http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ Archived Toake Shrine Website] (Japanese, archived) * {{ill|Tomei Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomei Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Kitakyūshū City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyūshū City|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}) disambiguation]] [[Tajikarao Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Kitakyushu]] [[Category:Wakamatsu-ku]] [[Category:Shrines by deity]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tomei Shrine]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Tajikarao Shrine]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tomei Shrine]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:北九州市]] [[ja:福岡県]] [[sn:Tomei Shrine]] [[sn:omei Shrine]] [[ja:手力雄神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

{{Unreferenceds|date=September 2020}} {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Toake Shrine | image = {{image requested|cat=Kita-Kyushu City}} | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ward|qq=}}, 866 Azusumi | coordinates = {{coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} | former status = Village shrine | annual festival = {{ill|October 8|ja|10月8日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 8|qq=}} - {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 9|qq=}} | ceremonies = Setsubun Yakuyoke Festival, Bean-throwing Ceremony }} '''Toake Shrine''' (戸明神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Wakamatsu Ward|ja|若松区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamatsu Ward|qq=}}, {{ill|Kitakyushu|ja|北九州市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitakyushu|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, who appear in Japanese mythology. It is one of the few shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture dedicated to these deities. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine worships {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|アメノコヤネ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, deities who appear in the myth of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|天の岩戸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|qq=}}. == History == Toake Shrine serves as the main shrine (hongū) for the "Toake Shrines" located in Anyō, Arige, Ōtorii, and {{ill|Takasu, Kitakyushu|ja|高須 (北九州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu, Kitakyushu|qq=}}. It has long been revered as the general shrine (sōsha) for 18 {{ill|Ubusunagami Shrines|ja|産土氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami Shrines|qq=}} located in Inakuni, Iwaya, Wakita, Takenami, Harakawa, Shioya, and Koshiki. The exact date of its founding is unknown, but the ''Toake Shrine Engishojo'' (戸明神社縁起書序), recorded by the 6th head priest, Hatakake Motonari, in 1536 (Tenmon 5), states: *"Long ago, this shrine stood at Toake Beach, between Iwaya and Kashiwabara, and was built in an imposing and dignified manner. During the Kyōroku era, it was relocated to its current site."* From this, it is believed that the shrine was moved to its current location in Azusumi around 1528 (Kyōroku era) and that it had already existed before this relocation. According to the ''Fukuoka Prefecture Shrine Records'' (published in 1944), *"In ancient times, this was a grand shrine, counted among the three great shrines of the region. The thirty villages of the island district regularly performed prayers for the harvest in both spring and autumn, along with frequent special ceremonies throughout the year."* Considering that the shrine’s priestly family also held the title of head priest for the district, it is evident that the shrine once flourished as a center of religious activity. == External Links == *{{Wayback|url=http://www5.ocn.ne.jp/~toake/ |title=Official website of Toake Shrine |date=2007-12-30 05:38:13}}{{Dead link|date=May 14, 2018|title=http://toake.sakura.ne.jp/ exists but with a slightly different address.}} {{translated page|ja|戸明神社|version=79507045|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[File:Takou-jinja1.jpg|250px]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kitakyushu]] [[Category:History of Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Wakamatsu Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Togami Shrine]] [[ja:戸明神社]] [[ja:%E6%88%B8%E6%98%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Togami Shrine]] ==Merged second translation== {{出典の明記|date=2020th year 9月}} {{Shrine | name = Toake Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=北九州市}} | location = 福岡県北九州市若松区蜑住866番地 | coordinates = {{Coord|33|54|48.9|N|130|42|23.6|E|region:JP}} | Enshrined Deities = [[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Okami]]
